turns.user_message: I tried doing those guided breathing exercises again tonight, but my chest just felt too tight to actually take a full breath. It has been happening on and off for months now, and I am honestly so tired of pretending everything is fine. I made myself a cup of tea and sat quietly, hoping the warmth would finally settle my nerves, but my hands just kept shaking. Every time I close my eyes, my thoughts just bounce around like they are trapped in a cage. I really thought today would be different, but the heavy feeling in my stomach just refuses to leave me alone.

I keep trying to distract myself by listening to those calm nature sounds, but the static in my head completely drowns them out. This whole cycle has been dragging on for so long that I barely remember what a quiet mind actually feels like. I even went for a long walk yesterday hoping the fresh air would clear the fog, but my legs just felt heavy and restless the entire time. It is exhausting to constantly monitor my own heartbeat and wonder when the next wave will hit. I just want to switch my brain off for five minutes without feeling like I am losing my grip.

I tried every single grounding trick I could find online, counting tiles and focusing on objects, but nothing stops the sudden rush that hits me out of nowhere. It has been building up for weeks now, and I am completely worn out from fighting a battle inside my own head. I sat on the floor for an hour trying to steady my breathing, but my lungs just kept catching and my whole body felt like it was vibrating. The panic comes so fast that I can never prepare for it, and I am terrified of when it will strike next. I just feel completely trapped in this endless loop of dread that refuses to let me catch my breath.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a failed attempt to feel better. This has been going on for a while.
